codeReview.summary: The bootstrap commit satisfies the feature contract: it establishes a lightweight React + TypeScript + Vite shell, keeps project scripts aligned with `.factory/services.yaml`, uses relative build paths, and preserves the legacy deterministic board and local trie assets through a static bridge.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance requires targeted `file://` checks, but the worker discovered that the available Playwright browser tool in this environment blocks direct navigation to `file://` URLs. That limitation and the expected fallback are not documented in `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/pg/.factory/missions/0425d037-2167-448e-a74b-ed8e0a0ba29e/worker-transcripts.jsonl:1` records `playwright___browser_navigate` failing with `Access to "file:" protocol is blocked` for `file:///Users/pg/Coding/_bucket/oggleb/dist/index.html?...`, while `/Users/pg/Coding/_bucket/oggleb/.factory/library/user-testing.md` still instructs targeted `file://` checks without noting this tooling constraint.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature transcript skeleton, handoff, skill procedure, and commit `23af302`, and found the bootstrap foundation implementation aligned with the feature's required static app setup and legacy-preservation goals. I noted one shared-state knowledge gap about `file://` browser-tool limitations in this environment.
